title: Support boolean and/or in sequence expressions
body:
## Summary

This patch adds support for boolean `and`/`or` operators within SVA sequence expressions (IEEE 1800-2023, 16.9.2). These operators were unconditionally rejected at parse time with `BBUNSUP`, even when both operands are simple expressions (non-temporal), where the existing `AstLogAnd`/`AstLogOr` mapping is already correct. Temporal (sequence-level) `and`/`or` operands remain caught downstream by V3Width.

## Reproducer

```systemverilog
module t (input clk);
  logic a, b, c;
  assert property (@(posedge clk) (a and b) |-> c);
  assert property (@(posedge clk) (a or b) |-> c);
endmodule
```

On master:

```
%Error-UNSUPPORTED: t.v:3:40: Unsupported: and (in sequence expression)
    3 |   assert property (@(posedge clk) (a and b) |-> c);
      |                                        ^~~
                    ... For error description see https://verilator.org/warn/UNSUPPORTED?v=5.047
%Error-UNSUPPORTED: t.v:4:40: Unsupported: or (in sequence expression)
    4 |   assert property (@(posedge clk) (a or b) |-> c);
      |                                      ^~
%Error: Exiting due to 2 error(s)
```

## Changes

- `src/verilog.y`: Remove `BBUNSUP` calls from `sexpr yAND sexpr` and `sexpr yOR sexpr` productions; the `AstLogAnd`/`AstLogOr` nodes they already create are correct for boolean operands
- Updated golden files `t_property_unsup.out`, `t_sequence_first_match_unsup.out`, `t_sequence_sexpr_unsup.out` to remove the now-resolved `and`/`or` unsupported messages

## Test

- Added `test_regress/t/t_sequence_bool_ops.{v,py}` -- runtime assertion test with boolean `and`/`or` in sequence context, including constant operand variants
- Updated `test_regress/t/t_property_unsup.out` -- removed 2 `or (in sequence expression)` errors now resolved
- Updated `test_regress/t/t_sequence_first_match_unsup.out` -- removed 4 `or (in sequence expression)` errors now resolved
- Updated `test_regress/t/t_sequence_sexpr_unsup.out` -- removed `and` and `or (in sequence expression)` errors now resolved

---
Developed by PlanV GmbH, assisted with Claude Code.

Reviewed by YilouWang.
****
